diagrams.title: Wireframe Fidelity Comparison
diagrams.description: Comparison of low-fidelity, mid-fidelity, and high-fidelity wireframes showing progressive detail levels
elements: Lo-Fi: Simple boxes and lines (hand-drawn or basic shapes)
Mid-Fi: Digital shapes with spacing, grayscale, basic typography
Hi-Fi: Detailed layout with placeholder images, consistent spacing, interactive elements
Color: All grayscale (purposefully no color)
Content: Placeholder text (Lorem ipsum)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the progression of wireframe fidelity and when to use each type

diagrams.title: Wireframe vs Mockup vs Prototype Flow
diagrams.description: Visual representation showing the progression from wireframe to mockup to prototype
elements: Wireframe: Skeleton - grayscale layout showing structure only
Mockup: Static visual - colors, images, typography applied
Prototype: Interactive - clickable, simulates user flows with animations
Arrows showing progression from one to next
Dotted lines showing iteration loops back to earlier stages
diagrams.purpose: Clarifying the distinct purposes and differences between these three design deliverables

diagrams.title: Wireframe Layout Elements Template
diagrams.description: Standard website wireframe layout showing common elements placement
elements: Header: Logo (left), Navigation (center/right), Search
Hero Section: Large content area for main message
Sidebar: Optional secondary content
Content Area: Main text/images in grid
CTA Buttons: Prominent call-to-action elements
Forms: Input fields with labels
Footer: Links, copyright, social icons
Annotations: Numbered callouts for explanations
diagrams.purpose: Reference template for creating balanced, user-friendly wireframe layouts

diagrams.title: Responsive Wireframe Variations
diagrams.description: Wireframe layouts showing how the same content adapts across mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports
elements: Desktop: Multi-column horizontal layout (sidebar + main content)
Tablet: Adjusted columns, touch-friendly sizing
Mobile: Single column stacked layout, hamburger menu
Navigation: Full menu → condensed → hamburger icon
Content: Multiple columns → single column with sections
Touch targets: Minimum 44px for mobile tap targets
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how to plan responsive layouts in wireframes for multi-device support
